channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: France vs Spain: High-Stakes World Cup Semifinal Preview
item.description: The FIFA World Cup semifinals on July 15, 2026, will see France face Spain in a highly anticipated clash on Bastille Day. Both teams are among the top-ranked sides globally, with France boasting Ousma...

item.title: Globacom Recognizes Top Dealers with New Cars and Prizes at 2026 Awards Ceremony
item.description: Globacom celebrated its top dealers at the 2026 Glo Dealers’ Award Ceremony by rewarding them with brand-new cars, premium tech gadgets, and other valuable prizes. The event, hosted by media personali...

item.title: Maria Matray: A Star of Weimar Cinema and Hollywood Choreographer
item.description: Maria Matray, born Maria Charlotte Stern in 1907, was a German-Jewish dancer, actress, and screenwriter who rose to fame in the 1920s and 1930s. She gained prominence as a star of late Weimar cinema, ...

item.title: Moreland Council Lists Liquidations for July 13
item.description: The article discusses a list of businesses undergoing liquidation within the Moreland Council area for July 13. However, the provided text appears to be an error message from News Corp Australia, indi...

item.title: Former Manitoba MP Charged with Firearms Trafficking After Police Seize Over 400 Weapons
item.description: A former federal politician denies trafficking any firearms but acknowledges illegally transferring three guns after police seized 439 weapons from his western Manitoba home. Inky Mark, 78, was charge...

item.title: Study Links Sleep Deprivation to Increased Road Accident Risk in South Africa
item.description: A study by Discovery Insure has revealed that drivers with chronic sleep deprivation, or 'sleep debt', are significantly more likely to be involved in road accidents. The research analysed four years ...

item.title: Gordie Howe Bridge Opening Deal Sparks Concerns Over Transparency
item.description: The article details the ongoing negotiations for the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge, highlighting concerns from local officials about the lack of transparency in the agreement. Canadi...

item.title: NHS Delay in Treating Cancer-Stricken Grandmother Leads to Worsening Condition
item.description: Marie Stibbe, a 79-year-old grandmother from Devon, was diagnosed with liver cancer in March 2026. Despite experiencing severe symptoms, including itchy legs and diabetes, her NHS treatment was delaye...

item.title: Extreme Heat Threatens Europe's Nuclear Energy Security
item.description: France is reducing nuclear power output at up to five plants due to record-high temperatures that have raised river levels beyond safe cooling thresholds for reactors. This follows a deadly July heatw...

item.title: Former DPWH Chief Bonoan Faces Graft Charges in Sandiganbayan Trial

item.title: US approves self-injectable lecanemab for early Alzheimer's disease, raising hopes for future NHS availability
item.description: The US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has approved a self-injectable version of lecanemab for people with early Alzheimer's disease, marking a significant development in the treatment of the condi...

item.title: Lagos Police Hunt for Killers of NURTW Secretary Toba Ajiboye

item.title: Nuclear Plant Safety Concerns Arise Over Alleged Data Manipulation by Japanese Utility
item.description: A recent report suggests that Chubu Electric Power Co., a major utility in Japan, may have manipulated earthquake resistance data for its nuclear facilities following a significant seismic event in 20...

item.title: Kansas Governor Appoints Liberal Judge, Expanding Democratic Influence on State Supreme Court
item.description: Kansas Governor-elect Laura Kelly has appointed Chris Jayaram, a liberal judge, to replace retiring Justice Marla Luckert on the Kansas Supreme Court. This move increases Democratic control of the sta...

item.title: Tanzanian Mine Workers Report Labor Rights Violations and Unsafe Conditions at Chinese-Owned Site
item.description: Workers at Tianpin Investment Management Ltd, a Chinese-owned gold mine in Kahama District, Tanzania, have accused the company of labor rights violations and unsafe working conditions. The complaints ...

item.title: US military escalates tensions with Iran as Trump reverses Hormuz fee plan
item.description: The US has intensified military operations against Iran following President Donald Trump's abrupt reversal of a proposed 20 per cent levy on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz. The decision co...

item.title: Spain takes 1-0 halftime lead over France after Oyarzabal penalty
item.description: In a thrilling FIFA World Cup 2026 clash, Spain secured a 1-0 half-time lead over France after a crucial penalty awarded to them. The match, played on Tuesday, July 14, 2026, saw Spain forward Lamine ...

item.title: Lagos police dismantle car fraud syndicate, recover stolen vehicles
item.description: The Lagos State Police Command has successfully dismantled a car fraud syndicate and recovered stolen vehicles, urging dealers to exercise caution. The operation targeted individuals involved in illeg...

item.title: Folarin Balogun addresses FIFA ban overturn controversy after long silence
